TracetoForge Guide & FAQ Open Editor Blog

TracetoForge: Turn Any Tool Photo Into a Custom-Fit Insert

The fastest way to create custom tool storage. Snap a photo of any tool, auto-trace the outline, and export STL, 3MF, SVG, or DXF files for 3D printing, laser cutting, or CNC machining. Works with Milwaukee Packout, Gridfinity, DeWalt ToughSystem, Ridgid Pro Gear, and any toolbox. No CAD skills needed.

100% browser-based. Nothing to install. Free to start.

Start Building

How It Works: Four Steps, Two Minutes, Done

Step 1: Snap a Photo

Place your tool on a sheet of paper. Take a photo with your phone. Any contrasting background works. No scanning equipment needed.

Step 2: Trace and Adjust

TracetoForge edge detection finds the tool outline automatically using OpenCV.js. Fine-tune with simple click-and-drag controls. Set real-world dimensions, tolerances, and depth with sliders.

Step 3: 3D Preview

See your insert in full 3D before exporting. Rotate, zoom, and inspect fit, finger notches, and dimensions. Verify everything looks right before committing.

Step 4: Export and Print

Download STL, 3MF, SVG, or DXF files. Slice it and hit print. Works with any 3D printer, laser cutter, or CNC router including Creality, Bambu Lab, Prusa, xTool, Glowforge, Shapeoko, and more.

Three Ways to Forge: Insert Output Modes

Custom Tray Insert

Rectangular or oval tray with a precision-cut cavity for your tool. Set wall height, thickness, and edge chamfers. The most popular option for tool chest drawers, workbench organizers, and toolbox inserts.

Gridfinity Bin Insert

Standard Gridfinity-compatible insert with proper 42mm grid base profile, stacking lip, and grid alignment. Drops right into your Gridfinity baseplate. The open-source modular storage standard.

3D Object Export

Just the extruded shape of your tool outline. Perfect for custom mounts, templates, jigs, or prototyping parts. Use as a base for more complex CAD projects.

Four Export Formats, One Click

Every design exports in four industry-standard file formats. Pick one or download all as a zip.

STL - 3D Printing

The universal 3D print format. Works with every slicer and every FDM or resin printer. Compatible with Cura, PrusaSlicer, OrcaSlicer, and Bambu Studio.

3MF - Modern 3D Print Format

The next-generation 3D print format preferred by Bambu Lab, PrusaSlicer, and OrcaSlicer. Smaller file sizes and richer metadata than STL.

SVG - Laser Cutting and Vinyl

Vector outlines ready for laser cutters, vinyl cutters, and Cricut machines. Cut foam, felt, leather, or acrylic inserts. Perfect for kaizen foam replacement.

DXF - CAD and CNC Machining

Industry standard for CNC routers, AutoCAD, and Fusion 360. Mill wood, HDPE, or aluminum tool trays on Shapeoko, X-Carve, or Onefinity machines.

Simple Pricing, No Subscription

Start free. Pay only when you export. Credits never expire.

Free Tier - $0

3 free export credits on signup. Full editor access. All 3 output modes. STL, 3MF, SVG, and DXF exports. Unlimited tracing and previewing. No credit card required.

Starter Pack - $9.99

5 export credits at $2.00 per export. Credits never expire. All output modes and formats. Priority support.

Pro Pack - $34.99 (Best Value)

20 export credits at $1.75 per export. Credits never expire. All output modes and formats. Priority support. Best price per export.

Tracing and previewing are always free. You only use a credit when you export a file.

Built for Makers, Not Engineers

You should not need a CAD degree to organize your toolbox. TracetoForge turns anyone with a phone camera into a custom insert designer.

Problem: Spent 4 hours learning Fusion 360 just to make one tray. Solution: TracetoForge does it in under 2 minutes.

Problem: Hand-cutting foam with a knife looks terrible. Solution: Get precision inserts from a photo. Print, laser cut, or machine them.

Problem: Downloaded a generic bin that does not fit your tools. Solution: Every insert is custom-shaped to your exact tools.

Problem: Paid $40 or more for a single pre-made Packout insert. Solution: Print unlimited custom inserts for pennies in filament.

Works With What You Already Own

Export files sized for your exact organizer system. Pick your brand, set your case dimensions, and print, cut, or machine inserts that fit perfectly.

Primary Tool Storage Systems

Milwaukee Packout: The industry standard with the largest ecosystem of cases, mounts, and accessories. DeWalt ToughSystem 2.0: Heavy-duty modular storage with a big presence at Home Depot. DeWalt TSTAK: Compact and lightweight, great for mobile setups.

Growing Tool Storage Systems

Ridgid Pro Gear 2.0: Home Depot exclusive modular storage. Makita MakTrak: New and growing fast. Flex Stack Pack: Lowe's powerhouse modular system. Gridfinity: The 42mm open-source modular standard.

Additional Compatible Systems

Klein ModBox for electricians. Bosch L-Boxx contractor system. Festool Systainer premium woodworking storage. Makita MakPac Systainer-compatible cases. Also works with Kobalt, Husky, Craftsman, Stanley FatMax, Harbor Freight, and Snap-on tool storage.

Compatible Hardware

Any 3D Printer: FDM, Resin, or SLA including Creality, Bambu Lab, Prusa, Elegoo, and Anycubic. Any Slicer: Cura, PrusaSlicer, OrcaSlicer, Bambu Studio. Laser Cutters: xTool, Glowforge, K40, CO2 lasers. CNC Routers: Shapeoko, X-Carve, Onefinity, and more.

Custom Inserts for Any Tool

Whether it is a tool chest, drawer, tray, or modular organizer, TracetoForge creates custom tool storage that fits like a shadow board. Works for pliers, screwdrivers, socket sets, ratchets, wrenches, utility knives, hex keys, allen wrenches, wire strippers, multimeters, drill bits, tape measures, chisels, files, flashlights, levels, clamps, and hammers.

Works as a foam organizer alternative. Replace kaizen foam with precision 3D printed, laser cut, or CNC machined inserts for your tool drawer, tool chest, workbench, or garage storage setup. Use as socket holders, drawer dividers, or custom shadow boards.

Your Photos Never Leave Your Browser

All image processing happens locally on your device using OpenCV.js. No uploads, no cloud processing, no accounts required to trace. Your tool photos and designs stay on your machine. TracetoForge respects your privacy.

Stop Cutting Foam. Start Forging.

From photo to ready-to-make files in under two minutes. 3D print, laser cut, or CNC machine custom tool inserts. No sign-up required to start.

Open Editor

Frequently Asked Questions

Is TracetoForge free to use?

Yes. Tracing and previewing are always free. You get 3 free export credits when you sign up. After that, credit packs start at $9.99 for 5 exports.

Do I need CAD experience?

No. TracetoForge is designed for makers, not engineers. Take a photo of your tool, and the app handles the rest. No CAD, no 3D modeling, no technical skills required.

What file formats can I export?

STL for 3D printing, 3MF for modern slicers like Bambu Studio and PrusaSlicer, SVG for laser cutters and vinyl cutters, and DXF for CNC routers and CAD software like Fusion 360.

Does it work with Milwaukee Packout?

Yes. TracetoForge supports Milwaukee Packout case dimensions and generates inserts sized to fit Packout organizers, compact organizers, and toolboxes. Also supports DeWalt ToughSystem, Gridfinity, Ridgid Pro Gear, and many more.

What is Gridfinity?

Gridfinity is an open-source modular storage system based on a 42mm grid. TracetoForge generates Gridfinity-compatible bins with proper base profiles and stacking lips that drop into any Gridfinity baseplate.

Are my photos uploaded to a server?

No. All image processing happens locally in your browser using OpenCV.js. Your photos never leave your device. Only the final export files are generated server-side when you use a credit.

What 3D printers work with TracetoForge?

Any 3D printer that accepts STL or 3MF files. This includes all Creality printers (Ender-3, K1, K2), Bambu Lab (A1, P1S, X1C), Prusa (MK4, Mini), Elegoo, Anycubic, and any other FDM or resin printer.
